frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: gradual integration of noisy sensory evidence over seconds toward a decision

frameworks.name: cortical hierarchy of intrinsic timescales
frameworks.one_line: distributed cortical areas with distinct integration windows jointly support accumulation

core_question: Is recruitment of widespread cortical areas during evidence accumulation organized by their intrinsic integration timescales?
key_assumptions: cortical areas have stable intrinsic timescales forming a hierarchy
perceptual decisions require gradual accrual of sensory evidence over time
brief optogenetic silencing isolates an area's contribution within a trial epoch
manipulations: cortical area silenced (many areas across the hierarchy)
timing of brief inactivation across trial epochs
key_findings: inactivation of every tested area affected the accumulation computation
frontal inactivations produced stronger deficits at long timescales than posterior ones
some frontal silencings also altered behavioral processes beyond accumulation
wide-field Ca2+ activity revealed distinct temporal integration windows across areas
interpretations: intrinsic timescale hierarchy of distributed cortical areas is a core component of evidence-accumulation mechanisms
